data.title: Ruby Receptionist: Real Humans + AI for Every Call
data.excerpt: Ruby answers your calls and chats 24/7 with real people backed by AI. Never miss a lead again. See how Ruby works for your business.

$media.alt: Ruby Receptionist: Real Humans + AI for Every Call

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through our links, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.*

There is one sound that makes every small business owner wince: a phone ringing with nobody to pick it up. Maybe you are in a meeting, on another line, or finally sitting down to dinner. That missed call could be the client who was ready to sign a contract, the patient who needed an appointment, or the homeowner searching for a quote.

Ruby solves this problem in a way that feels almost unfair to your competitors. It is a virtual receptionist platform that combines trained, real human agents with AI-powered technology to answer every call and web chat your business receives — 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, 365 days a year. The result? Every person who contacts you feels like they reached a friendly, knowledgeable member of your own team.

I first came across Ruby while looking for a solution for a client running a mid-size law firm. Their intake process was a mess. Calls went to voicemail after hours, and even during business days, the front desk was overwhelmed. Within a week of setting up Ruby, their missed-call rate dropped to nearly zero. That is not a hypothetical. That is what happens when real people pick up the phone every single time.
Check out Ruby's pricing and plans here
What Exactly Is Ruby?
Ruby is a business answering service available through
PartnerStack

. But calling it just an "answering service" undersells what it does. Think of it as an extension of your team that never calls in sick, never takes a holiday, and always sounds like they have been working in your office for years.

Here is how it works. When someone calls your business number, Ruby's system routes the call to a live receptionist. That receptionist has been trained on your specific business — your greeting style, your frequently asked questions, your intake procedures, and even how to handle tricky callers. The caller hears a professional, warm voice that sounds like they dialed directly into your office.

The AI layer sits behind the scenes. It handles intelligent call routing, real-time transcription, sentiment detection, and seamless integration with your CRM or scheduling tools. Ruby's AI does not replace the human element — it amplifies it. The technology gives receptionists better context, faster access to your business information, and the ability to handle complex scenarios with confidence.

Web chats follow the same philosophy. A visitor lands on your website, clicks the chat widget, and within seconds, a real person is typing back. No chatbot loops, no "I'm sorry, I didn't understand that." Just a person helping another person.
Features That Stand Out
Ruby packs a surprising amount of capability into a service that feels effortless on your end. Here are the features I find most impressive:
Live Answering 24/7/365
— This is Ruby's bread and butter. No voicemail, no hold music, no "our office is closed." A real human picks up every time, whether it is 2 PM on a Tuesday or 3 AM on Christmas morning.
Bilingual Support

— Ruby offers English and Spanish receptionists, which opens the door to a massive segment of callers that many businesses accidentally ignore. This alone can be a competitive advantage depending on your market.
Custom Call Handling

— You decide exactly how calls are managed. Want to screen certain callers? Transfer emergencies to your cell? Take messages only for specific departments? Ruby builds a script around your preferences and trains their team accordingly.
Web Chat with Real Agents

— Ruby is not limited to phone calls. Their web chat service puts a live person on your website, ready to engage visitors, answer questions, and capture leads. This is especially powerful for service-based businesses where a quick response can be the difference between winning and losing a customer.
CRM and Software Integrations

— Ruby connects with popular tools like Clio, Salesforce, HubSpot, and others. Call details, messages, and chat transcripts flow directly into the systems you already use, so nothing falls through the cracks.
Smart Mobile App
— The Ruby app lets you manage your availability, view messages, check call history, and adjust settings from your phone. You stay in control without being tethered to a desk.
Appointment Scheduling

— Ruby receptionists can book appointments directly into your calendar during the call. No back-and-forth emails. No missed scheduling windows. Just a confirmed appointment on your calendar before you even know the call happened.
Spam and Solicitation Blocking
— Ruby filters out junk calls so you only hear about the ones that matter. Your time stays protected.
If you want a deeper walkthrough, our
step-by-step Ruby tutorial
covers setup and configuration in detail.
Why Ruby Feels Different from Other Answering Services

I have tested a lot of phone answering solutions. Automated attendants, overseas call centers, AI-only chatbots. Most of them fail at the same point: the caller knows they are not talking to someone who actually cares about your business.

Ruby avoids that problem completely. Their receptionists are based in the United States and undergo extensive training — not just on Ruby's systems, but on each individual client's business. When someone calls a Ruby-powered business, the experience genuinely feels like speaking with an in-house team member.

The AI technology makes this possible at scale. Without it, training hundreds of receptionists on thousands of unique businesses would be impractical. But Ruby's AI surfaces the right information at the right moment, so a receptionist can handle a nuanced question about your pricing or direct a caller to the correct department without hesitation.

This human-plus-AI approach is what sets Ruby apart. Neither element works as well alone. A chatbot without empathy frustrates callers. A human without context gives generic answers. Together, they create something that feels remarkably seamless.
For a broader comparison, see our guide on
why Ruby is worth choosing
See Ruby in action and start your free trial
Who Is Ruby Ideal For?
Ruby serves a wide range of businesses, but it shines brightest for specific types of organizations:
Law Firms and Legal Practices

— Legal intake is time-sensitive and often happens outside business hours. Ruby captures those leads, screens potential clients, and routes urgent matters to the right attorney. The bilingual support is especially valuable in family law and immigration practices.
Medical and Dental Offices

— Patients calling to schedule appointments, ask about insurance, or report urgent issues need a live person, not a recording. Ruby handles overflow during peak hours and covers after-hours calls flawlessly.
Home Service Businesses
— Plumbers, electricians, HVAC companies, and landscapers spend their days on job sites. Ruby ensures that the phone is always answered, quotes get scheduled, and new leads do not go to a competitor.
Solo Entrepreneurs and Consultants
— When you are your entire business, every minute on the phone is a minute not spent delivering work. Ruby acts as your virtual front desk so you can focus on what you do best.
Growing Teams That Are Not Ready to Hire a Full-Time Receptionist
— A dedicated receptionist costs $30,000 to $45,000 per year before benefits. Ruby gives you a team of trained professionals for a fraction of that cost, with zero HR overhead.
E-Commerce and Online Businesses
— Even digital-first companies need a human voice sometimes. Ruby's web chat service captures website visitors who might otherwise bounce, turning browsers into buyers.
Want more ideas? Explore our collection of
Ruby use cases
to see how businesses like yours are using it.
How to Get Started with Ruby
Getting started is refreshingly simple. You sign up on
Ruby's website

, choose a plan based on your expected call volume, and go through a brief onboarding process. During onboarding, you provide details about your business: how you want calls handled, what information receptionists should collect, and how to route different types of calls.

Ruby assigns you a dedicated number (or ports your existing number) and within a day or two, your virtual receptionist team is live. You can test it yourself by calling in and seeing how natural the experience feels.
The Ruby mobile app becomes your command center. You can toggle your availability on and off, review call summaries, read chat transcripts, and adjust your call-handling preferences in real time.

Most businesses report feeling the impact immediately. Missed calls drop. New leads get captured. Clients comment on how professional and responsive the phone experience has become. It is one of those operational upgrades that makes you wonder how you managed without it.
Our
Ruby tips and best practices guide
has more advice on getting the most out of your setup from day one.
New Features Worth Knowing About

Ruby continues to evolve its platform. Recent updates include enhanced AI capabilities for better call routing and context delivery, deeper CRM integrations, and improved analytics that let you track call volume, peak hours, and conversion patterns.

Their web chat feature has also received significant upgrades, with faster response times and more customization options for matching the chat widget to your brand. The bilingual support has expanded in quality, with more receptionists fluent in Spanish and better AI-assisted translation tools for edge cases.
Stay current with all the latest by checking our
Ruby new features roundup
What Real Users Say

Ruby consistently earns strong reviews on platforms like Trustpilot. Business owners frequently praise the professionalism of the receptionists, the ease of setup, and the tangible impact on their bottom line. Common themes in reviews include:
"My clients think they are talking to someone in my office."
"We stopped missing calls entirely."
"The setup took less time than I expected."
"Worth every penny for the peace of mind."
The recurring sentiment is that Ruby does not just answer phones — it elevates the entire customer experience.
The Bottom Line

If your business depends on phone calls or web inquiries — and most do — Ruby is one of the smartest investments you can make. It gives you the reliability of 24/7 coverage, the warmth of a real human voice, and the intelligence of AI-powered technology, all without the cost and complexity of hiring in-house staff.
Whether you are a solo practitioner who needs to stop missing calls or a growing firm that wants to deliver a five-star first impression on every interaction, Ruby adapts to fit your needs.
I genuinely believe this is one of those tools that pays for itself within the first month. The leads you capture, the clients you retain, and the time you reclaim all add up fast.
Start your Ruby free trial today and see the difference

children.text: How much does Ruby receptionist cost?

Ruby offers several pricing plans based on your expected call volume. Plans start with a set number of receptionist minutes per month, with options to scale up as your business grows. Visit Ruby's website to see current pricing and choose the plan that fits your needs.
Does Ruby use real people or just AI?

Ruby uses both. Every call and chat is handled by a trained, real human receptionist. AI works behind the scenes to deliver context, route calls intelligently, and transcribe conversations — but the person speaking with your caller is always a real human.
Can Ruby answer calls in Spanish?

Yes. Ruby offers bilingual receptionist support in English and Spanish. This is especially valuable for businesses serving diverse communities, such as law firms, medical practices, and home service companies.
How quickly can I start using Ruby after signing up?

Most businesses are up and running within one to two business days. The onboarding process includes a brief call to configure your call-handling preferences, and Ruby assigns or ports your number quickly so you can start receiving calls right away.
